subject: DevOps
title: Netflix Christmas Eve Outage (2012)
description: A quick, practical guide to Netflix Christmas Eve Outage (2012).

hook.text: December 24, 2012. Families sat down to stream. Netflix was down. An AWS ELB issue was the cause.

buildup.text: Netflix depended on a single AWS region. When us-east-1 degraded, there was no fallback.

discovery.text: The single-region dependency was the single point of failure. No multi-region failover existed.

twist.text: After this, Netflix invested heavily in active-active multi-region architecture.

climax.text: They built Chaos Kong to simulate losing an entire region. Resilience became a core value.

punchline.text: One region is one point of failure. Go multi-region.

quiz.question: What key change did Netflix make after the 2012 outage?
options: They switched away from AWS entirely
